title: Frame loom tapestry wall hanging
subtitle: A 20 x 30 cm woven wall piece using tabby and simple geometric colour blocks on a frame loom
excerpt: A frame loom needs no mechanical parts: it is a simple rectangle of wood or metal with notches or nails along the top and bottom to tension the warp. This wall hanging uses tabby weave and simple geometric blocks in four colours to produce a 20 x 30 cm piece. It is a first weaving project that establishes the fundamentals: warping, weaving tabby, joining colour sections, and finishing the edges.
sourceNotes: Frame loom tabby weaving is the entry-point technique for the craft. Method is observation-led and consistent with the approach in numerous contemporary weaving guides.

content.text: with colour blocks: horizontal bands of alternating weft colours. The
keeps the selvedges straight. The finished piece measures approximately 20 x 30 cm, hanging on a 25 cm wooden dowel through the top loops of the warp fringe.

items.name: Warp yarn (smooth cotton or linen at DK weight)
Weft yarn in 4 colours (wool at DK-aran weight)

content.text: Tie the warp yarn to the top-left nail. Bring it down to the bottom-left nail, around the nail, back up to the next nail on the top, and so on, zigzagging down and up to warp the full width. Aim for 4-5 warp ends per cm (10-12 per inch). Keep tension consistent throughout: all ends should ping at a similar pitch when plucked.
Weave four picks of the warp yarn itself as a header at the base of the loom. This spreads the warp threads to their correct spacing before the decorative weft begins.

Pass the first weft colour through the shed (every other warp thread). Leave a weft arc, then beat gently with the shuttle or comb. Alternate the shed for the next pick (the opposite warp threads go up). Weave 15-20 picks of the first colour, then change to the second colour. Do not cut: simply start the new colour from the selvedge, leaving a 5 cm tail to weave in later.

When the piece is the desired height, weave four more picks of warp yarn as a finishing header. Cut the warp off the loom, leaving 8-10 cm of fringe. Thread the top warp ends over the dowel and tie them in pairs with a simple overhand knot to secure. Weave in any loose weft tails by threading them onto a blunt needle and running them under two or three adjacent weft picks on the reverse side.

items.fix: Leave a larger arc: at least a quarter of the row width. Press the arc down gently with a fingertip before beating.
items.cause: Weft pulled too tightly before beating, with no arc
items.problem: The edges curve inward as weaving progresses

items.fix: Leave 5 cm tails and weave them in on the reverse, running under 3-4 adjacent picks. Trim close after weaving in.
items.cause: Tails left too short or woven in from the front
items.problem: Weft tails visible on the front of the piece

items.fix: Always start a new colour from the same selvedge (left or right, be consistent) and leave a tail to weave in.
items.cause: The new colour started from the middle of the row rather than from the selvedge
items.problem: The colour bands have uneven edges where colours change
